items.q: Do waves erode cliffs evenly along the whole coast?
items.correct: No — erosion concentrates where rock is weaker, joints and faults exist, and wave energy is highest, creating headlands, bays, caves, arches and stacks.
items.misconception: Coastlines retreat at one uniform rate everywhere.
items.random_wrong: Cliffs erode only during a full moon.
items.why: Differential erosion explains nearly every coastal landform on this specification.

items.q: Is a river's load only moved when it floods?
items.correct: No — rivers transport material continually by traction, saltation, suspension and solution; capacity rises with discharge, so floods simply move much more, much faster.
items.misconception: Sediment transport happens solely during flood events.
items.random_wrong: Rivers carry sediment uphill at night.
items.why: Continuous transport processes underpin landforms from V-shaped valleys to floodplains and levees.

items.q: Did glaciers carve UK valleys by melting the rock away?
items.correct: No — glaciers eroded through abrasion, with embedded rock fragments scraping the bed, and plucking, freezing onto and tearing out blocks, creating corries, arêtes and U-shaped valleys.
items.misconception: Ice melted or dissolved the rock to form glacial valleys.
items.random_wrong: Glacial valleys were dug by early farmers.
items.why: Naming the actual erosion mechanisms is required for glaciated upland landform questions.

items.q: Which line divides upland and lowland Britain?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Tees-Exe Line. “Watford Line” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Where are softer sedimentary rocks found in UK?
items.why: Correct answer: Southeast England. “Scottish Highlands” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Approximately how many km of coastline does the UK have?
items.why: Exact answer: 17820 km. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
